commands.name: blackjack
commands.summary: Play blackjack — solo vs the dealer, or challenge a player (stakes escrowed).

commands.name: bjtournament
commands.summary: Start a Blackjack tournament (button + reaction registration).

commands.name: bjstart
commands.summary: Manually start a pending Blackjack tournament early.

body.text: Beat the dealer without going over 21. Free play wins pay a fixed reward; bets pay 1:1 (naturals 3:2). Challenge a player with `!blackjack @player [bet]` — stakes are escrowed when the challenge is accepted.

panels.justification: the shipped table embed is game-state-parameterized copy (Dealer (N+?)/Your hand (N)/Bet fields with a mixed inline layout and a result-keyed accent color — views/blackjack/embeds._game_embed); grammar TextBlocks are static. Buttons and authority stay declared on the spec; the renderer only composes the embed + declared components with their state-derived disabled flags.
panels.title: 🃏 Blackjack

panels.justification: the shipped challenge/match/result embeds are match-parameterized copy on every line (challenger/target mentions, bet line, per-player card reveals — views/blackjack/pvp_view.py); grammar TextBlocks are static, and the buttons are g1: dynamic-session components minted from the match's session id (never static panel ids). Authority stays on the g1 dispatcher table's declared specs; the renderer only composes the staged embed + buttons.
panels.title: 🃏 Blackjack Challenge!

panels.justification: the shipped registration embed's five field values are request-parameterized copy (fee/rounds/mins arguments + the live player count and pot — views/blackjack/embeds._tourn_embed); grammar TextBlocks are static. The Join button and its authority stay declared on the spec; the renderer composes the embed + the declared button and asks for the shipped ✅ self-reaction primer.
panels.title: 🃏 Blackjack Tournament — Registration Open

panels.justification: the shipped tournament round embed is game-state-parameterized copy (the _game_embed card fields plus the _finish_round `Chips: **N** | Rounds left: **N**` result field); grammar TextBlocks are static. Buttons and authority stay declared on the spec; the renderer only composes the embed + declared components with their state-derived disabled flags and the entrant's invoker lock.
panels.title: 🃏 Blackjack Tournament

panels.justification: every line of the shipped results embed is tournament-parameterized copy (medal icons, member names, chip counts, the payout amount/balance field — views/blackjack/tournament_views._check_tourn_done); grammar TextBlocks are static, and the frame carries zero components.
panels.title: 🏆 Blackjack Tournament Results

settings.hint: Default entry fee (🪙 coins) applied when an admin runs `!bjtournament` without an explicit entry_fee argument.
settings.name: default_entry_fee

panels.justification: the shipped hub embed is live-data-parameterized (dataset version/counts + per-kind freshness lines) with the command legend + ` • ctx=btd6_hub:main` footer — outside FooterMode's vocabulary and the static TextBlock grammar. The override delegates the component rows to the grammar renderer and replaces ONLY the embed (goldens/btd6/sweep_btd6menu pins every byte).
panels.title: 🐵 BTD6 Assistant

panels.justification: the shipped `!btd6 <sub>` replies are fully live-data-parameterized embeds built by sb/domain/btd6/oracle_cards.py (colors/footers/fields outside the grammar vocabulary — goldens/btd6 pins the bytes). Zero components; the renderer presents the handler-built RenderedEmbed verbatim.

the shipped CT-team embed carries the ` • ctx=btd6_ct:team` footer and a Manage-Server-only button on a discord.py auto-minted id (goldens/btd6/sweep_btd6_ctteam pins the <cid:1> mix) — the renderer gates the button on the opener's operator fact and delegates minting to the session engine.
panels.title: 🛡️ BTD6 — Your CT Team

commands.name: chain
commands.summary: Manage message chains and word limits.

commands.name: create
commands.summary: Lock a channel to one allowed word.

commands.name: delete
commands.summary: Remove a channel's chain (word and limit).

commands.name: setlimit
commands.summary: Cap messages at N words (0 removes the cap).

commands.name: list
commands.summary: List every chain/limit in this server.

body.text: Message chains: lock a channel to one allowed word, cap message length, and watch the chain count climb. All other messages are removed.

Select an action below to manage your server's channels.

**➕ Create Channel** — interactive channel creator
**🗑️ Delete Channel** — select and delete a channel
**🔒 Manage Restrictions** — lock or unlock a channel
**↔️ Move / Reorder** — bulk-move channels or send to top/bottom

panels.justification: the shipped panel footer is the literal author-lock notice 'Only the command author can interact with this panel.' (main_panel.build_embed set_footer) — outside FooterMode's none/subsystem/provenance vocabulary (goldens/channel/sweep_channelmenu pins the byte; the utility/ux_lab-panel precedent). The override delegates to the grammar renderer and replaces ONLY the footer; body, actions and layout stay declared.
panels.title: 🛠️ Channel Management Panel

body.text: Auto-moderation policies for command-style messages and prohibited content. Per-channel behaviour is configured under **Cleanup Policies** below.

panels.justification: the shipped hub footer is the literal 'Read-only summary. Use the buttons below to manage policies.' (cogs/cleanup/panel.py set_footer) — outside FooterMode's none/subsystem/provenance vocabulary — and the shipped Prohibited Words count field renders inline=True (panel.py add_field(inline=True)) — outside the grammar's vocabulary (2-tuple fields render inline=False). goldens/cleanup/sweep_cleanup.json pins both bytes (the settings-hub/access precedent). The override delegates to the grammar renderer and adjusts ONLY those two surfaces; body, fields, actions and layout stay declared.
panels.title: 🧹 Cleanup Hub

panels.justification: the shipped words-manager footer is the literal 'Use buttons below to manage prohibited words.' (cleanup_cog.py set_footer) — outside FooterMode's none/subsystem/provenance vocabulary (goldens/cleanup/sweep_wordmenu.json pins the byte; the settings/server_management footer precedent). The override delegates to the grammar renderer and replaces ONLY the footer; body, fields, actions and layout stay declared.
panels.title: 🔤 Prohibited Words Manager

commands.summary: Toggle reset-to-start on a wrong count.

selectors.placeholder: Enable Here — pick a counting mode

commands.name: catch
commands.summary: Hunt a wild creature — rarity-weighted encounter, level-nudged catch roll.

body.text: Hunt wild creatures — rarity drives both how often they appear and how hard they are to catch. Your creature level (shared game XP) nudges the odds.

commands.name: dm_challenge
commands.summary: Challenge another user to a deathmatch duel.

body.text: Turn-based duels: 100 HP each, attack for 15 (30 on a crit), defend to halve the next hit. Challenge a player with `!deathmatch @user` — PvP wins count on the leaderboard. **Fight Bot** starts an immediate duel vs the bot (results stay off the leaderboard).

settings.hint: Seconds each player has to respond on their turn before the duel times out and the opponent wins by default.
settings.name: turn_timeout

Choose a job below.
Pay increases **+1%** each time you work the same job (max +100%).

selectors.empty_state: ❌ No jobs available. Earn XP or buy items from 🛒 Shop.

body.text: Buy items to unlock higher-tier jobs. Purchases are unique — one of each per member.

commands.summary: Cast a line — one instant catch roll.

commands.summary: Your fish dex — every species you've caught.

commands.summary: Set sail to the deepwater venue.

body.text: Cast a line — 21 species across 7 size bands; level up to unlock bigger fish. Caught fish land in your pack (sell or cook them); the biggest of each species is your trophy.

commands.summary: Open the Games hub — competitive games and channel activities.

commands.name: world
commands.summary: Open the Explore world hub — the open-world town square.

commands.name: worldcard
commands.summary: Show your cross-game world card — global level + per-game standing.

body.text: Competitive games and channel activities. Wagered games escrow stakes when a challenge is accepted; your cross-game progress lives on the shared world track (`!worldcard`).
The open-world town square. Each game keeps its own ladder; your shared world level derives from the game-XP pool. Game worlds (Mine · Fish · Farm · Creatures) dock here as their bands land.

commands.name: bugreport
commands.summary: Report a bug — Hermes dispatches a Claude Code session to fix it automatically.

commands.name: dispatch
commands.summary: Send a raw Hermes work order to the Claude Code Routine (owner only).

body.text:
The Hermes→Claude dispatch bridge is not configured.

Set these env vars on Railway (or in `.env` locally):
```
CLAUDE_ROUTINE_FIRE_URL=https://api.anthropic.com/v1/claude_code/routines/<id>/fire
CLAUDE_ROUTINE_TOKEN=sk-ant-oat01-…
CLAUDE_ROUTINE_BETA=experimental-cc-routine-2026-04-01
CLAUDE_ROUTINE_VERSION=2023-06-01
```
See `docs/operations/hermes-dispatch-bridge.md` for the full setup runbook.

panels.justification: the shipped `!pm <sub>` replies are fully live-data-parameterized embeds built by sb/domain/projmoon/oracle_cards.py (the provenance footer + the greyple footer-less miss cards sit outside the grammar vocabulary — goldens/project_moon pins the bytes). Zero components; the renderer presents the handler-built RenderedEmbed verbatim.

commands.name: +prize
commands.summary: Grant a winner exclusive access to the proof channel.

commands.name: -prize
commands.summary: End the prize session — proof channel read-only again.

commands.name: prizemenu
commands.summary: Open the interactive prize channel management panel.

commands.name: timedprize
commands.summary: Grant timed access; auto-unlocks after N minutes (survives restarts).

panels.justification: the shipped hub embed's description is STATE-dependent (proof_channel_cog.py build_embed: 'Managing {ch.mention}' when the proof channel resolves, the '⚠️ No `#proof` channel found. Create one first.' warning otherwise) and its footer is the literal 'Use buttons below to manage prize access.' (set_footer) — both outside the grammar's static-TextBlock/FooterMode vocabulary; goldens/proof_channel/sweep_prizemenu.json pins both bytes (the cleanup-hub precedent). The override delegates to the grammar renderer and adjusts ONLY those two surfaces (description + footer); actions, layout and frame stay declared. The shipped bound-branch 'Current Permissions' field renders LIVE channel overwrites (_format_overwrites(ch.overwrites)) — a Discord read with no capture twin and no pinning golden; it lands with the channel-ops slice (under-port note).

settings.hint: Channel used for prize-claim sessions (`+prize` / `timedprize` lock it to the winner). When bound it takes precedence over the legacy lookup of a channel literally named `proof`.

commands.name: rps
commands.summary: Quick RPS — vs the bot, or challenge a player (stakes escrowed at accept).

commands.name: rpsmatchup
commands.summary: Manually create a tournament match between two members.

commands.name: rpssettings
commands.summary: Update RPS settings (bare shows the current values).

body.text: Quick play vs the bot (pick a move below — free play wins pay a fixed reward), challenge a player with `!rps @player [bet]` (stakes escrowed at accept), or run tournaments with `!rpsregister` / `!rpsstart`.

panels.justification: the shipped quick-play view's bet line is request-parameterized copy (Bet: **N** 🪙 / free play — views/rps/solo_play.py); grammar TextBlocks are static. Buttons and authority stay declared on the spec; the renderer only composes the embed + declared components.
panels.title: ✂️ Rock · Paper · Scissors

panels.justification: the shipped challenge/result embeds are match-parameterized copy on every line (challenger/target mentions, bet line, per-player move reveals — views/rps/pvp_challenge.py + pvp_play.py); grammar TextBlocks are static, and the buttons are g1: dynamic-session components minted from the match's session id (never static panel ids). Authority stays on the g1 dispatcher table's declared specs; the renderer only composes the staged embed + buttons.
panels.title: ✂️ RPS Challenge!

panels.justification: the shipped registration embed's Entry Fee / Game Mode field values are request-parameterized copy (fee argument + the default_mode setting — cogs/rps_tournament_cog.rps_register); grammar TextBlocks are static. The Join button and its authority stay declared on the spec; the renderer composes the embed + the declared button and asks for the shipped ✅ self-reaction primer.
panels.title: 🎮 Rock Paper Scissors Tournament Registration 🎮

panels.justification: every line of the match view is match-parameterized copy (player mentions, mode/best-of, per-throw score reveals — the shipped match-channel sends); grammar TextBlocks are static. The mode decides WHICH declared move buttons render (classic 3 / lizard_spock 5 / chess 3 / elemental 3) — declared actions and authority stay on the spec; the renderer only composes the staged embed + the mode's declared subset.
panels.title: ✂️ RPS Tournament Match

The override delegates to the grammar renderer and replaces ONLY the footer; body, fields, actions and layout stay declared.

The override delegates to the grammar renderer and replaces ONLY the footer; body, fields, selector, actions and layout stay declared.

commands.name: setup

body.text: The setup wizard's sections, ordered by the registry (A-9). Interactive flows arm per-section as they port.

commands.name: treasury
commands.summary: Open the server treasury — view the pool and contribute coins.

commands.name: contribute
commands.summary: Donate your own coins into the server treasury.

commands.name: grant
commands.summary: Disburse coins from the treasury to a member (managers only).

body.text: The server's shared coin pool. Everyone can **Contribute** their own coins to grow it; server managers disburse from it with `!treasury grant @member <amount>`.

**📊 Poll** — create a reaction poll
**🔔 Remind Me** — set a timed reminder
**🔗 Invite** — generate a one-use server invite

body.text:
Browse every interaction pattern SuperBot could use. Every exhibit **reacts** when you press it and carries a spec card (use-for / avoid-for / platform limits).

**Nothing here is real**: the lab never writes to the database or changes the server (CI-enforced).

commands.name: xpmenu
commands.summary: Open the XP panel showing your rank and quick admin actions.

commands.name: rank
commands.summary: Show rank in a category (xp/coins/both or any leaderboard category).

commands.name: givexp
commands.summary: Give XP to a user (admin only).

commands.name: resetxp
commands.summary: Reset a user's XP to zero (admin only).

commands.name: xpimport
commands.summary: Import XP/levels from another bot's level-up channel (raise-only, preview first).

body.text: Chat to earn XP and level up. `!rank` shows your card; admins configure via the settings hub.

panels.justification: the shipped hub send carries the invoker's rank image card as the message attachment `rank.png` (views/xp/main_panel.py build_response — the visual card-engine H3 surface, oracle PR #1413) — attachments are outside the grammar's block vocabulary; goldens/xp/sweep_xpmenu.json pins the multipart send shape ({"_files": ["rank.png"]}) and the preceding avatar `get_from_cdn` read. The override delegates to the grammar renderer and adds ONLY the attachment (avatar fetch + placeholder card bytes — rank_card.py); embed, fields, actions and layout stay declared.
panels.title: 🏆 XP Panel

panels.justification: the shipped `!rank` reply is the rank image card sent as the message attachment `rank.png` (utils/rank_render.py over services/xp_helpers.build_rank_response) — attachments are outside the grammar's block vocabulary; goldens/xp/xp_chat_award.json pins the multipart send shape ({"_files": ["rank.png"]}) and the preceding avatar `get_from_cdn` read. Zero components; the renderer composes only the attachment (the utility.profile_card precedent).
